flow_overview: A cruise website landing-page flow for configuring and launching a cruise search using destination, departure port, month, and duration filters.

requirements.text: The system shall support cruise discovery through a single search workflow that combines destination, departure port, departure month, and trip duration criteria.
requirements.rationale: The ordered flow shows one search form being progressively configured across multiple criteria until a complete query is assembled.

requirements.text: The system shall allow the user to select a cruise destination or region from a browseable set of sailing destinations.
requirements.rationale: The destination selector expands into a panel of region options and the user chooses Alaska from that set.

requirements.text: The system shall retain the selected destination in the search controls while the user configures the rest of the query.
requirements.rationale: After Alaska is chosen, that value remains visible in the search form during the later port, date, and duration selection steps.

requirements.text: The system shall allow the user to choose a sail-from location from a list of departure ports.
requirements.rationale: The sail-from control expands into multiple departure locations and the flow selects San Francisco, CA.

requirements.text: The system shall preserve the selected departure port while subsequent search criteria are edited.
requirements.rationale: Once San Francisco, CA is selected, it stays visible in the search controls during later date and duration configuration.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to select sailing dates at month and year granularity across multiple years.
requirements.rationale: The date selector opens a month grid organized by year, showing selectable months across 2023, 2024, and 2025.

requirements.text: The system shall keep the selected sailing month and year visible as part of the active search query.
requirements.rationale: After June 2023 is selected, that value remains displayed in the date field during the remaining search configuration steps.

requirements.text: The system shall allow cruise searches to be filtered by duration bands such as 2 to 5 days, 6 to 9 days, and 10 plus days.
requirements.rationale: The duration control exposes multiple duration categories and the flow selects the 10 plus days option.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a search action that the user can invoke after configuring cruise criteria.
requirements.rationale: A prominent Search Cruises action is visible throughout the flow and remains available after the query is configured.

requirements.text: The system shall keep the currently configured search criteria visible together before search execution so the user can review the query state.
requirements.rationale: As selections accumulate, the search bar shows destination, port, date, and duration together, supporting visible review before submission.

requirements.text: The system shall provide clear visual feedback when a selector is expanded and when an option is currently active or selected.
requirements.rationale: Expanded selectors are visibly highlighted and chosen options are marked through active styling and check-state indicators.

requirements.text: The system shall allow the user to revise any individual search criterion before submitting the search.
requirements.rationale: The search controls remain present and individually accessible after earlier selections, supporting stepwise refinement before execution.

requirements.text: The system shall return cruise results that reflect the combined selected destination, departure port, month, and duration after the search is submitted.
requirements.rationale: The visible flow is clearly constructing a search query for later execution, so results constrained by the chosen filters are strongly implied even though the result page is not shown.

requirements.text: The system shall expose only genuinely searchable or available itinerary options for the user's query context when presenting destination, port, date, and duration choices.
requirements.rationale: The selectable option panels imply inventory-backed search constraints, but screenshots alone cannot verify whether the offered options are actually correct or complete.

requirements.text: The system shall preserve already selected cruise search criteria when the user leaves the search flow and returns later in the same browsing session.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: persistence_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-03
Source gold text: The system shall retain the selected destination in the search controls while the user configures the rest of the query.
Generation rationale: This is a close persistence extension of the existing carry-over behavior for selected criteria, but it strengthens that behavior beyond the immediately visible configuration sequence into later return behavior within the same session. That makes it realistic for travel search while likely harder to settle fully from a short ordered screenshot flow.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall retain the selected destination in the search controls while the user configures the rest of the query.

requirements.text: The system shall present search results only for sailings that are currently available under the combined selected destination, departure port, sailing month, and duration filters, and shall show user-facing feedback when no matching cruises are available.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: hidden_state_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-13
Source gold text: The system shall return cruise results that reflect the combined selected destination, departure port, month, and duration after the search is submitted.
Generation rationale: This extends the result-reflects-query requirement with a realistic availability condition and no-results feedback, both common in cruise search. The visible core remains close to the original results behavior, but actual inventory correctness depends on hidden backend state, which makes partial fulfillment or uncertainty plausible.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall return cruise results that reflect the combined selected destination, departure port, month, and duration after the search is submitted.

requirements.text: The system shall return all cruises that satisfy the configured destination, departure port, sailing month, and duration criteria, without omitting any eligible sailings.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: completeness_or_universal_quantifier
Source gold requirement: REQ-13
Source gold text: The system shall return cruise results that reflect the combined selected destination, departure port, month, and duration after the search is submitted.
Generation rationale: This is a disciplined completeness strengthening of the source gold requirement. It stays in the same search-results capability space, but it turns a plausible visible requirement into a universal coverage claim that ordered screenshots alone usually cannot prove, making abstention a realistic later outcome.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall return cruise results that reflect the combined selected destination, departure port, month, and duration after the search is submitted.

requirements.text: The system shall provide the same cruise search criteria and equivalent search behavior to anonymous users and signed-in users unless a visible policy restriction is communicated.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: policy_or_role_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-01
Source gold text: The system shall support cruise discovery through a single search workflow that combines destination, departure port, departure month, and trip duration criteria.
Generation rationale: This adds a realistic account and access-policy constraint to the same core search workflow without drifting into unrelated admin behavior. It remains close to the user-facing cruise discovery feature, but it likely cannot be resolved from a single unauthenticated flow unless multiple account states are shown.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall support cruise discovery through a single search workflow that combines destination, departure port, departure month, and trip duration criteria.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated pre-search review step or summary panel that lists all selected cruise criteria together and allows the user to edit any one of them before submitting the search.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: missing_visible_step
Source gold requirement: REQ-10
Source gold text: The system shall keep the currently configured search criteria visible together before search execution so the user can review the query state.
Generation rationale: This is a realistic strengthening of the existing reviewability requirement into an explicit workflow phase rather than merely keeping criteria visible inline. It stays tightly anchored to the same search configuration flow, but such a dedicated review step is often absent in streamlined travel search UIs, making not fulfilled plausible.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall keep the currently configured search criteria visible together before search execution so the user can review the query state.

requirements.text: The system shall allow the user to filter cruises by an exact itinerary length in days in addition to broad duration bands.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: nearby_capability_variant
Source gold requirement: REQ-08
Source gold text: The system shall allow cruise searches to be filtered by duration bands such as 2 to 5 days, 6 to 9 days, and 10 plus days.
Generation rationale: This is a disciplined nearby variant of the duration-filtering capability that remains within the same cruise search feature space while changing the granularity of filtering. It is stronger than the accepted band-based requirement and is a plausible human-authored requirement for this task, while still likely to fall outside the shown capability.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall allow cruise searches to be filtered by duration bands such as 2 to 5 days, 6 to 9 days, and 10 plus days.
